title: Process Signals, Abort Trees, Listener Cleanup, and Graceful Shutdown · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A public science-learning team incrementally builds composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle from original fixed PokéAPI payloads while retaining prior TypeScript, HTTP, Git, repository-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Translate approved process signals into one root AbortController in the executable shell and define first and repeated signal behavior.
Compose session, command, timeout, and waiter signals while preserving reason and avoiding controller ownership confusion.
Use once or signal-bound listeners, remove handlers on completion, and prove no listener-count growth across commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3587 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove signal-to-abort translation, composed reasons, listener cleanup, ordered close, and state safety; unknown external values must not cross the runtime validator; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Shutdown Tests through the concrete composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test abort before and during question, fetch, cache wait, prefetch, and save plus repeated signal, cleanup order, state, and output.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Shutdown Tests precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6875 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove signal-to-abort translation, composed reasons, listener cleanup, ordered close, and state safety; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use budget must remain enforced; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test abort before and during question, fetch, cache wait, prefetch, and save plus repeated signal, cleanup order, state, and output.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A manual Ctrl-C run proves all async resources terminate.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: pokedex-ts-shutdown-tests-5cuheb.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: pokedex-ts-shutdown-tests-5cuheb(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_pokedex_ts_shutdown_tests_5cuheb\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ownedTasks < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?settledTasks !== ownedTasks)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?listenerDelta !== 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stateLosses > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
